label: Page topic or summary
placeholder: Describe what the page contains, offers, or explains.
helper: Summarize the real page content so the metadata accurately represents it.
why: The model must understand the actual page before it can write accurate metadata.

label: Page type
placeholder: Select the closest page type.
helper: Page type changes the title structure, description focus, slug, and social metadata.
why: Different page types require different metadata priorities and search-intent treatment.

label: Target audience
placeholder: Example: Small business owners looking for simple invoicing software
helper: Describe the people most likely to search for and benefit from this page.
why: Audience context determines which benefits, terms, and intent signals should be emphasized.

label: Primary keyword or query
placeholder: Example: online invoice generator
helper: Enter the main phrase the page should naturally target.
why: The primary query anchors the title, description, and slug without requiring keyword stuffing.

label: Essential page facts
placeholder: List real features, benefits, locations, prices, dates, or other facts that may be mentioned.
helper: Only provide facts that are accurate and publicly supported by the page.
why: It supplies the factual basis and prevents the model from inventing claims or page details.

label: Primary page goal
placeholder: Select the page’s main goal.
helper: The goal influences the description angle and the strength of any action phrase.
why: Pages targeting the same keyword may require different metadata depending on their conversion purpose.

label: Output language
placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish
helper: Metadata will be written in this language and writing system.
why: The output language cannot always be safely inferred from a short topic or keyword.

label: Brand or site name
placeholder: Example: Creatornivo
helper: Used only when brand inclusion improves recognition or is explicitly requested.
why: Brand names cannot be invented and may materially affect title length and structure.

label: Page URL
placeholder: https://example.com/page
helper: Used for canonical and social tags when it is the intended public URL.
why: A real URL enables implementation-ready canonical and social metadata without fabrication.

label: Search intent
placeholder: Let the model infer intent or choose one.
helper: Use Auto unless the page deliberately targets a particular stage of the search journey.
why: Explicit intent can prevent informational and commercial pages from receiving the wrong metadata angle.

label: Supporting keywords
placeholder: Add closely related phrases, one per line or separated by commas.
helper: Only include relevant phrases that may naturally support the primary topic.
why: Supporting phrases can improve topical coverage when used selectively.

label: Geographic target
placeholder: Example: Toronto, Canada or nationwide United Kingdom
helper: Complete this only when location materially affects the page or search intent.
why: Local relevance must come from the user rather than being inferred or invented.

label: Keyword and wording notes
placeholder: List required terms, preferred wording, or phrases that must not appear.
helper: Combine important inclusion and exclusion instructions in one place.
why: It captures material language restrictions without creating several overlapping fields.

label: Brand in SEO title
placeholder: Choose how the brand should be handled.
helper: Auto includes the brand only when useful and space allows.
why: Brand inclusion affects title clarity, distinctiveness, and available length.

label: Slug preference
placeholder: Select how the URL path should be handled.
helper: Choose whether to create, shorten, revise, or preserve the current slug.
why: Slug recommendations differ significantly between new and already published pages.

label: Current slug or path
placeholder: Example: /services/online-invoice-generator
helper: Enter the existing path so it can be reviewed or preserved.
why: The current path is necessary only when the user wants it reviewed or retained.
display: Show when `slugPreference` is `Improve existing slug` or `Keep existing slug`.

label: Indexing preference
placeholder: Use Auto unless you know the page should be restricted.
helper: Controls whether a robots meta recommendation is included.
why: Indexing directives can materially change page visibility and should not be guessed casually.

label: Canonical URL
placeholder: https://example.com/preferred-page
helper: Complete this when the preferred canonical differs from or must override the page URL.
why: Canonical URLs are technical factual values that must never be fabricated.

label: Include social metadata
placeholder: Blank
helper: Enable this to generate Open Graph or X Card preview metadata.
why: Social tags are valuable for some pages but unnecessary for users who only need search metadata.

label: Social metadata platforms
placeholder: Select one or both metadata formats.
helper: Open Graph supports broad social sharing; X Card adds X-specific tags.
why: The selected platforms determine which social tags should be generated.

label: Social preview image URL
placeholder: https://example.com/images/page-preview.jpg
helper: Use an absolute public image URL; the model will not invent one.
why: Social preview images require an actual hosted URL rather than a generated filename.

label: Social image description
placeholder: Describe what is visibly shown in the social preview image.
helper: Describe only real visible content and avoid keyword stuffing.
why: A supplied image description supports accurate and accessible social metadata.

label: Number of variants
placeholder: Choose how many title and description pairs to generate.
helper: More variants provide alternative angles without changing the factual basis.
why: Users may need either one final answer or several options for review.

label: Additional requirements
placeholder: Add any final content, formatting, CMS, or implementation requirements.
helper: Use this only for important instructions not already covered above.
why: It accommodates legitimate edge cases without expanding the permanent form.
